brands.notes:
Method: mined lower("extractedBrand") top ~400 frequencies AND leading 1-token / 2-token title phrases (freq thresholds 8-20 and >=40), then spot-checked sample titles for each uncertain token to separate true manufacturer/marketing brands from ingredient-identity words. Transliterated Serbian (č/ć->c, š->s, ž->z, đ->dj) but ALSO kept original-diacritic and common ASCII spelling variants in the list (e.g. josif pancic / josif pančić, l'oréal / loreal / l'oreal) because titles in the DB are inconsistently transliterated — strip-matching should be done after the same normalization you apply to titles.

CRITICAL caveats for the grouping pipeline:
1. The ambiguous list is the most important output. Many "brands" in extractedBrand are actually INGREDIENTS or generic descriptors and must NEVER be blindly stripped, or you destroy the identity: vitamin, magnezijum/magnesium, cink, omega, kolagen/collagen, protein, whey, bcaa, kreatin/creatine, glutamine, propolis, aloe, koenzim/q10, glukozamin, hijaluron, probiotik, melatonin, ashwagandha, biotin, pantenol, etc. E.g. "Collagen", "Omega 3", "Magnezijum 24h", "Ashwagandha 450mg", "Probiotic 10 kapsula" are full product identities whose first token is the ingredient.

2. Generic prefix/marketing words are AMBIGUOUS, not brands: now, super, ultra, pure, active, smart, max, top, natural, multi, plus, strong, vital, pro, complete, daily, power, essence, food(s), bio, vegan, forte, direct, junior, kids, premium, complex/kompleks, multivitamin, 100%, iso. They lead some brand names (e.g. "Max Factor", "Pure Baby", "Strong Nature", "Active B Complex") but are also legitimate identity/strength descriptors. Strip them only when they appear as a leading token immediately followed by another brand token, never standalone. Note the current hardcoded list wrongly contains generic words now/super/ultra/multi/essence/food/plus/nutrition as hard brands — that risks over-stripping.

3. Brand-named-products (detrical, videtril, soldevit, oligovit, beviplex, supradyn, centrum) ARE single-vendor product names, not cross-brand manufacturers. They are in BOTH lists deliberately: they are the only identity those rows have (e.g. "Videtril 2000IJ" is a vitamin-D brand). If you strip them you must remap to the canonical ingredient (e.g. videtril/detrical/soldevit -> "vitamin d3", beviplex/oligovit -> "b complex / multivitamin"), otherwise leave them as identity. Do NOT strip-to-empty.

4. Multi-token brand prefixes need phrase matching, longest-first: "la roche-posay", "l'oreal paris", "maybelline new york", "strong nature", "natural wealth", "bivits activa", "josif pancic", "max factor", "now foods", "terra nova"/"terranova", "dr theiss/hauschka/grandel/luigi/browns", "oral b", "head & shoulders". Match the 2-3 word phrase before falling back to single tokens.

5. "elixir" is a real brand here (nail-care line) but "a-lixir/elixa/elixy" are different brands and "elixir" also appears generically; treated elixir as brand but flag related forms as ambiguous.

6. Tokens like dr/aj/jp/la/oral are NOT standalone strippable — only as part of a brand phrase (dr <name>, la roche, oral b, jp <product>). "aj" almost always means "broj/article" filler in čaj listings, not a brand.

Coverage: ~360 brand entries (incl. spelling variants) spanning cosmetics, derma/pharmacy, baby, supplements/sports-nutrition, oral care, perfume houses, pet food, and tea/herbal vendors; ~200 ambiguous entries covering ingredients + generic descriptors + brand-named-products. Source field of interest in code: ml/populate_missing_data.py _extract_core_ingredient() and the hardcoded brand list it uses.

ingredients.notes:
SCOPE: 84 canonical identities covering the highest-frequency supplement, cosmetic-active, and OTC-active families. I capped at the truly high-signal, defensible identities rather than padding to 150 with noise tokens. The remaining top-500 coreProductIdentity rows are dominated by NON-INGREDIENT noise that must be stripped, not aliased: form words (prašak, šumeće, tablete, kapsule, sirup, krema, gel, melem, stik, roll-on, češalj/četkica, maramice), variant/marketing words (control, sensitive, strong, smart, fresh, repair, junior, complex, kompleks, formula, advance, intense), pack/size tokens (60kap, 90kap, 100kap, 90c, 60c, doza, broj, grama), and brands (eucerin, uriage, bioderma, avene, sebamed, mustela, l'oreal/l' paris, nivea, becutan, sudocrem, galenika, maxmedica, dietpharm, now, ostrovit, ervamatin, pampers, etc.). These belong in a separate STOPWORD/BRAND list, not the alias map.

DATA OBSERVATIONS: dosage coverage is only ~4.9% (5109/104780) and form ~41% (43060) confirming the brief. Cyrillic is rare in titles (only 11 rows, and those are mostly stray Cyrillic letters inside otherwise-Latin words like "kremа"/"Мixa"). I still included Cyrillic aliases for the major ingredients because they appear in user search queries even if not in the catalog. ALL outputs are diacritic-transliterated to ASCII canonicals (gvozdje not gvožđe, maticnjak not matičnjak, africka sljiva), with the diacritic forms kept as aliases for matching.

MERGES I MADE (per your guidance that buyers treat as same):
- magnezijum: ALL salts (citrat, bisglicinat/glicinat, malat, hlorid, oksid, taurat) folded into one identity. Strength still separates them via dosageValue/dosageUnit in the group key. FLAG: bisglycinate vs oxide have very different bioavailability; some power-buyers care. If you want salt-level granularity later, split these out — they are individually frequent (citrat ~52, bisglicinat ~49, hlorid ~28, malat ~13).
- vitamin d3: holekalciferol/cholecalciferol folded in (same molecule). Plain "vitamin d" mapped here too since in this catalog "vitamin d" almost always means D3; FLAG if you stock vitamin D2/ergocalciferol separately — those should NOT map here.
- vitamin c: acerola, ester-c, liposomal-c, askorbinska all folded (same identity, different delivery). Retard/buffered are just forms.
- vitamin b3: niacin + niacinamid folded as supplement identity. FLAG: as a COSMETIC active "niacinamid" is a separate identity (kept as canonical "niacinamid", category cosmetic). Disambiguate by category/context or these two will collide — niacinamid appears in both a serum and a B-vitamin pill.

DELIBERATELY KEPT SEPARATE (would be costly wrong-merges):
- "vitamin d3 k2" / "d3+k2" / "d3+zn" combos are a DISTINCT identity from plain d3 (~80+ combo rows). Do NOT collapse into vitamin d3.
- "magnezijum b6" kept separate from magnezijum (it is a specific co-formulation, ~36+ rows).
- "b kompleks" kept separate from individual B-vitamins and from multivitamin.
- whey protein vs vegan/biljni protein kept separate (different buyers, allergen-relevant).
- omega 3 vs omega 3-6-9: I FOLDED 3-6-9 into omega 3 (common practice, fish-oil shelf) but FLAG it — purists consider 3-6-9 distinct (adds plant oils). Easy to split if needed.

PROBIOTIC CAUTION (important): I created ONE generic "probiotik" identity, but the catalog's probiotics are overwhelmingly sold as BRANDED single/multi-strain products (Bulardi=S.boulardii, Flonivin=B.subtilis, Flobian, Enterobiotik, BioGaia=L.reuteri, Linex, etc.). These are clinically NOT interchangeable (different strains/indications). Do NOT auto-merge all probiotic brands into "probiotik" for grouping — that is a high-cost wrong merge. Recommend keeping probiotics grouped by brand/strain, or adding strain to the key. The generic "probiotik" alias is safe only for query expansion, not for collapsing the group key.

OTHER FLAGS:
- "vitamin a" alias includes retinil/retinol palmitat (supplement form) but cosmetic "retinol" is its own canonical — keep separated by category to avoid merging a face serum with a vitamin A capsule.
- "biotin" sometimes appears as "vitamin h" / "vitamin b7/b8" — folded in; safe.
- mineral salt forms (cink pikolinat/glukonat, kalcijum karbonat/citrat, hrom pikolinat) folded into their base mineral; same bioavailability caveat as magnesium but lower stakes.
- IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: apply longest-alias-first matching (e.g. match "magnezijum b6" and "vitamin d3 k2" BEFORE "magnezijum"/"d3"), and require word-boundaries for short/ambiguous aliases ("mg","b6","b12","c","e","a","q10","aha","bha") to avoid catastrophic false matches (e.g. "mg" the unit, "c" inside other words). These short tokens are the main precision risk in the whole map.

noise.notes:
METHOD: Exported all 104,727 non-null coreProductIdentity values + 104,780 titles to /tmp, tokenized in Python (SQL regexp_split mangles Serbian diacritics/Cyrillic — must use Python with UTF-8). Ranked ~2,600 distinct tokens by frequency; cross-checked against current _NOISE_WORDS/_FORM_WORDS lists in /Users/ahab/pharma-search/ml/populate_missing_data.py (lines 262-319). Returned ONLY additions.

TOP POLLUTERS confirmed in identities: "complex"/"kompleks" (591+371), "control"(539), "soft"(464), "fresh"(477), "color/boja"(553+488), "matte"(329), "muška"/"ženska"(367/270), "men"/"women"(406/171), "baby"(523), "junior"(372), "kids"(347), "protiv X"(1902 — e.g. "Protiv Opadanja", "Protiv Peruti" are pure descriptors), "tečni"(708), "doza"(83), count leftovers "60kap/90kap/100kap"(86/79/69), age "6m/18m"(231/107), "24h"(128), "5cm"(82).

KEY FINDINGS:
1. Count/size/age leftovers escape the regex (60kap, 90c, 30cps, 24h, 6m, 5cm, 3in1, 8u1, 6pak). The _clean_title regex at line 340 misses glued forms like "60kap" because it requires word boundary before digits in some cases and the "x?\d+\b" rules don't catch trailing letters. Recommend ADDING a regex to strip trailing \d+(kap|kom|c|cps|caps|m|h|cm|mm|u1|in1) BEFORE the word filter, in addition to the literal-token list I returned.
2. Form-word TYPOS present in data: tabeta, tabelta, tabela, table, kapusla, kapsu, capsula. Also plural/case forms (kapsulama, kapsulu, kremom, masti, rastvora, granule, dražeje, supozitorije, vaginalete, oriblete, efervete) — current list only has nominative singulars/plurals.
3. "protiv" + symptom/target ("opadanja","peruti","komaraca","hemoroida","strija","akni","bora","ojeda","upale","grčeva") = pure descriptors, never ingredient. Strongly recommend ALSO adding a rule: if identity starts with "protiv", it carries no ingredient — these collapse to symptom labels not ingredients.
4. Cosmetic color/shade words dominate makeup rows (matte, nude, color names crna/bela/plava/red/black/nude/beige etc.) — pure variant, exclude per spec.
5. Demographics (muška/ženska/men/women/baby/kids/junior/dečiji/adult/unisex/family/trudnice/dojilje) — variant per spec.

CAUTIONS / DELIBERATELY NOT ADDED (real ingredients that look like noise): "selen/selenium", "cink/zinc", "magnezijum/magnesium", "gvožđe/iron", "kalcijum/calcium", "hrom", "biotin", "lutein", "retinol", "kofein/caffeine", "kreatin/creatine", "kolagen/collagen", "propolis", "med/honey" (honey IS often an ingredient), "ugalj"(activated charcoal — ingredient), "urea/urea/uree"(active in dermatology), "msm","glukozamin","arginin","glutamin","taurin" — all real actives, KEPT. Also "hyaluron/ha"(active) KEPT. NOTE: "complex/kompleks" is borderline — "vitamin b complex" is arguably an identity; included per your explicit example list, but if over-collapsing, blacklist only when it is a trailing token after a real ingredient.

OVERLAP NOTE: A few words (e.g. "natural","organic","plus","pro","active","forte","max","ultra","super","premium","set","poklon","gratis","spf*") are ALREADY in the current list — I omitted those and returned only NEW additions, but included case/diacritic variants and inflected forms not yet covered.

missedDosagePatterns: "2000 I.U." / "100I.U." — trailing-period i.u. — 12 rows have NULL dosage. Current regex alternative `i\.u\.` is followed by `\b`, but after the final `.` a `\b` requires a following word char; "I.U. KAPSULE" has a space so it CAN match, yet "2000 I.U." at end-of-string or before space fails because the `.` is the last char (no word boundary). All 12 confirmed NULL. Add optional trailing-period handling and treat `i. u.`, `i.u`, `iu.` too.

"1.000IU" / "120.000mg" / "250.000ij" — DOT used as THOUSANDS separator, MISPARSED as decimal: 24 rows stored value <1000 (e.g. "5.000IU" stored as 5.0, "250.000ij" as 250.0, "120.000mg" as 120.0). These never group with true-strength siblings (5000 IU, 250000 IU). The regex `(\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?)` greedily reads `.000` as a fraction.

"1,000mg" / "10,000IU" / "60,000IU" — COMMA used as THOUSANDS separator, MISPARSED: 54 rows stored <1000 (e.g. "Tribulus 1,000 mg" -> 1.0 mg, "10,000mg" -> 10.0 mg). Same root cause: comma treated as decimal point. "Coenzyme Q10,100mg" even produced bogus 10.1 mg.
"80 000 I.U." / "250 000" — SPACE thousands separator — 63 rows; current regex stops at the first group (parses "80" or fails) and the second triplet is dropped, so strength is wrong/low.
"400ug" / "300 ug" — micrograms written as plain "ug" (ASCII u, no greek mu) — 4 rows NULL. Regex only has `mcg|μg|µg`; missing `ug`.

Multi-strength FIRST-MATCH leakage e.g. "VITAMIN K2 (MK7) 100mcg. + VITAMIN D3 5.000IU" stored 100 mcg (grabbed K2, not the headline D3). Also "5.000IU" combined with thousands-sep bug. For D3+K2 the PRIMARY ingredient strength (D3) should win, but regex `.search()` takes leftmost token regardless of which ingredient it belongs to.

Percent strengths like "5%","10%","2%" (retinol/AHA/BHA topicals) are not extracted at all as a strength dimension; 1382 rows contain `N%` but most are "100%" purity noise — needs a guard so only sub-100 single-ingredient % becomes strength.

gram-as-dosage threshold edge: 1080 rows stored dosageUnit='g'; the <=5g rule keeps 5g lip-balm/gel/tea weights AS dosage (e.g. "BALM ZA NOS 5G", "INSTANT ČAJ 5G") — these are pack weights, not active-ingredient strength, so they pollute the strength key.
volumePatterns: ml/l well covered: 41,294 titles contain `Nml`, 0 have NULL volumeValue — current VOLUME_RE works.

GRAM WEIGHT (>5g) is the biggest volume/size gap: 10,742 rows have `Ng`/`Ngr` weight (powders, teas, creams, protein, cosmetics e.g. "IsoWhey Powder 907 g", "COLLAGEN 300GR", "PASTA 15G") with NO volume captured. The pipeline only stores volume for ml/l; grams >5 are dropped entirely (not even moved to a weight field).
KG weight: 1,263 rows ("100% PURE WHEY 1KG", "2.27KG") have NULL volume — `kg` not in VOLUME_RE.
fl oz / oz (US NOW Foods imports): 23 rows e.g. "8 OZ", "12 oz (340 g)", "320ML/11OZ" — not parsed; most also carry a parenthetical ml/g that should be preferred.

Per-ml CONCENTRATION dosing "40mg/ml","500ij/8ml","200mg/5ml": 24 rows — currently the volume of the bottle (50ml) and the per-dose mg are both grabbed but the `/ml` ratio is not normalized; "Swiss+ D3 500ij/8ml" should key on D3 total/serving, not the 8ml denominator. Needs explicit ratio handling so the ml in the denominator is not mistaken for pack volume.
quantityPatterns: "90kap" — kap WITHOUT the s — 429 rows MISSED (quantityValue NULL). QUANTITY_UNIT_PATTERN has `kaps\w*|kapsula|kapsule` but bare `kap` (Serbian abbrev for kapsula) is not covered. Frequent: "Vitamin D3, 90kap", "Ashwagandha 500mg, 90kap".
"A30" / "A 60" prefix count — WORKS: 6,752 rows captured via QUANTITY_PREFIX_RE `[axх×](\d+)` (uppercase A matched case-insensitively). Confirmed quantityValue set.

"30X1000MG" / "60×1000mg" multipack — quantity MISHANDLED: 457 rows; the leading count (30, 60) is the pack size but it is consumed by the `NxN` strip in _clean_title and NOT recorded as quantity; worse, several got quantityValue=3 by grabbing "Omega 3" later ("CPS.30X1000MG" -> qty 3). The N before `x<number><unit>` should become quantity.

doza / lozenge(i) / pastila / supozitorija / vrećica counts — 308 rows MISSED (e.g. "ORTHOMOL IMMUN 15 DOZA", "24 lozenge", "10 supozitorija", "24 PASTILE"). These unit words are absent from QUANTITY_UNIT_PATTERN.
x60 / 60x bare count without unit: 229 rows contain `xN`; captured only when an A/a prefix; the `x`-prefixed bare counts that aren't NxN dosage are inconsistent.
examples: VITAMIN D3 2000 I.U. KAPSULE A30 (dosage NULL — trailing-period i.u.)
NOW® Foods Vitamin D3, 50.000 IU, 50 mekih kapsula (stored 50 iu, should be 50000)
SIWOPSI SERRAPEPTASE 250.000IU KAPSULE A 15 (stored 250 iu, should be 250000)
Tribulus 1,000 mg (stored 1.0 mg, should be 1000 mg)
Serrapeptase, 80 000 I.U. (space thousands + trailing period, NULL)
VITAMIN K2 (MK7) 100mcg. + VITAMIN D3 5.000IU 120 softgels Nutricost (stored 100 mcg — grabbed K2 not D3, and D3 thousands-sep wrong)
NUT.OMEGA 3 CPS.30X1000MG PHN (quantity stored as 3 from 'Omega 3', should be 30)
IsoWhey Fuel Powder 907 g (907g weight dropped, no size dimension)
ORTHOMOL IMMUN 15 DOZA (quantity NULL — 'doza' unit unrecognized)
Folat metilfolat 400ug 50 kapsula (dosage NULL — 'ug' micrograms not matched)
dosage.recommendations:
PRE-NORMALIZE the title string before any extraction, in this order: (1) Lowercase a working copy; map Cyrillic units (мг->mg, мкг->mcg, мл->ml, г->g, ме/ије->iu) and greek mu (µ/μ->mc). (2) STRIP THOUSANDS SEPARATORS before number parsing: collapse `(\\d{1,3})([.,\\s])(\\d{3})\\b` into `\\1\\2\\3` ONLY when the trailing group is exactly 3 digits AND the next char is a strength unit (iu|ij|mg|mcg|g) — this fixes 24 dot, 54 comma, 63 space cases without harming real decimals like 1,2mg. (3) THEN convert decimal comma->dot for genuine fractions `(\\d),(\\d{1,2})\\b`. 

DOSAGE regex (case-insensitive) — replace current with: `(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)?)\\s*(mg|mcg|ug|iu|ij|i\\.?\\s?u\\.?|i\\.?\\s?j\\.?|j\\.?\\s?m\\.?)\\.?(?![a-z])` using a negative lookahead `(?![a-z])` instead of `\\b` so trailing periods/end-of-string match (fixes 12 i.u. + makes ij/i.u./i.j./jm robust). Canonicalize units to a fixed set: {mg, mcg, iu, g}. Map ALL of {iu,ij,i.u.,i.j.,ije,jm,j.m.,ie} -> iu; {ug,µg,μg,mikrogram} -> mcg; {gr,gram,grama} -> g. 

UNIT CONVERSION for grouping: convert to a canonical base per ingredient — 1000 mcg = 1 mg only when comparing, but STORE original unit+value; the group key should canonicalize mcg->mg (value/1000) so \"5000mcg\" and \"5mg\" co-group, and keep iu separate (no mass equivalence). Round to avoid float noise (e.g. store as integer micrograms / milli-iu).

PICKING THE STRENGTH when multiple numbers: do NOT take leftmost blindly. Tokenize all (value,unit) pairs; (a) drop any pair whose unit is ml/l/g-weight or that is part of an NxN multipack; (b) if the title's core ingredient is a known single (D3, B12, C, magnesium...), pick the strength token adjacent to that ingredient; (c) for D3+K2 / multi-ingredient combos, pick the PRIMARY ingredient's strength (first ingredient in the canonical name), not the first numeric token — fixes the \"100mcg K2 grabbed instead of D3\" class; (d) ignore percent unless it is a sub-100 single value next to an active (retinol/AHA) — exclude \"100%\".

VOLUME/SIZE: extend VOLUME_RE to `(\\d+(?:[.,]\\d+)?)\\s*(ml|l|kg|g|gr|fl\\s?oz|oz)\\b`; route g/gr/kg to a WEIGHT dimension (or volumeUnit='g'/'kg') for the 10,742 g + 1,263 kg powder/tea/cosmetic rows so size shows as an offer attribute; convert kg->g and l->ml for canonical comparison; when both ml and oz present prefer metric. For `N mg/ml` concentration, capture the ratio separately and use the bottle ml (the standalone `Nml`) as pack volume, never the /ml denominator.

QUANTITY: (1) add bare `kap` to the unit alternation: `kaps\\w*|kapsula|kapsule|kap` (order longest-first; +429). (2) add count units: `doza|doze|lozeng\\w*|pastil\\w*|supozitorij\\w*|vre[cćČ]ic\\w*|sase\\w*|sachet\\w*|perlic\\w*` (+308+). (3) MULTIPACK: before stripping, match `(\\d{1,4})\\s*[xх×]\\s*\\d+(?:[.,]\\d+)?\\s*(mg|mcg|iu|ij|g)` and set quantityValue=group1 (the 30 in 30x1000mg), strengthValue=the inner number — fixes 457 rows where qty was wrong/NULL. (4) keep A-prefix and 'kom' (already working). 

NET IMPACT: these changes recover ~12 (i.u.) + ~141 (thousands-sep dot/comma/space) correctly-valued strengths, ~429 kap + ~308 count-word + ~457 multipack quantities, ~12,000 weight sizes, and prevent silent mis-grouping from the dot/comma decimal bug. Given dosage coverage is only 4.9%, also lower the strength-bearing universe assumption: only ~4,000 titles carry an explicit mg/mcg/iu token, so most products legitimately have no strength — the group key must tolerate NULL strength (ingredient-only group) rather than treating absence as a distinct strength.

cases.problem: PRECISION. searchTokens && overlap matches ANY product whose tokens contain 'vitamin' OR the single-letter 'c'. Returns ~2912 rows vs ~960 true 'vitamin c' titles. Pulls in Vitamin D3/E/A/B-6, Omega 3+Vitamin E, etc. The 1-char token 'c' is whitelisted in Tokenize() and appears on 1793 products, so it acts as a near-wildcard.
cases.expected: Top results must be actual Vitamin C products (e.g. 'BiVits ACTIVA Vitamin C 500 mg', 'BIOFAR Vitamin C 1000'). coreProductIdentity should equal 'Vitamin C'. Must NOT return Vitamin D3/E/A/B products that merely contain the letter 'c' token. Synergy products where C is secondary (e.g. 'Cink + Vitamin C') may appear lower but pure Vitamin C ranks first.

cases.problem: RECALL+PRECISION. (1) The hardcoded expansion 'mag'->'magnesium' is English while 669 DB titles are Serbian 'magnezijum' and only 277 are 'magnesium' — the abbreviation path misses the majority. (2) The dosage-unit token 'mg' collides: 3006 products carry an 'mg' token with no magnesium at all (e.g. 'ASPIRIN 20X500MG'), so any path keying on 'mg' explodes precision.
cases.expected: Return the ~942 magnesium products (magnezijum + magnesium unified). coreProductIdentity 'Magnezijum' and 'Magnesium' must group as one ingredient. Must NOT return products that only have a strength like '500mg' (aspirin, glukozamin). Serbian and English spelling must be treated as the same ingredient.

cases.problem: PRECISION. The numeric token '3' is on 2136 products; omega-OR-3 token overlap returns ~2304 rows vs ~450 true 'omega 3' titles. The bare number 3 (pack counts, '3 in 1', SPF, etc.) acts as a wildcard. Also identity fragmentation: omega products split across coreProductIdentity 'Omega'(143), 'Omega3'(19), 'Omega 90Kap', 'Dha Omega' — pack/form noise leaks into the key.
cases.expected: Return omega-3 fatty-acid products ('Omega 3', 'omega-3', 'omega3' all unified). coreProductIdentity should canonicalize to a single 'Omega 3' (strip pack size like '90Kap', strip 'Norveška'/brand). Must NOT return arbitrary products that merely contain the number 3.

cases.problem: RECALL is OK via expansion d3->'vitamin d3' (token d3 OR ilike 'vitamin d3' = 939, near the ~1024 true). But d3/d ambiguity: nothing distinguishes Vitamin D3 (431) from generic Vitamin D / D2 (584 total). Risk that 'd3' over-broadens to all 'vitamin d' or that the single-letter 'd' token (700 products) leaks via tokenization.
cases.expected: 'd3' resolves to Vitamin D3 specifically — return the ~431 D3 titles. Generic 'Vitamin D' (no number) should rank below or be a sibling, not be treated as identical strength. coreProductIdentity 'Vitamin D3' must be one canonical group regardless of brand (NOW/Solgar/Dacom).

cases.problem: RECALL. Expansion b12->'vitamin b12' and ilike '%b12%' covers compact spellings, but DB has spaced/hyphenated variants: 'Vitamin B 12', 'Vitamin B-12', 'B-12 Dots', 'NOW B-12'. NormalizeText collapses hyphens to spaces giving tokens [b,12] not [b12], so an ilike '%b12%' path misses 'b 12'/'b-12' titles. Combined b12-token-OR-ilike only ~276 rows.
cases.expected: Query 'b12' must match 'B12', 'B 12', 'B-12', 'Vitamin B12', 'Vitamin B-12 Methylcobalamin'. coreProductIdentity canonicalizes all to 'Vitamin B12'. Must NOT pull in unrelated B-complex / other B vitamins as exact matches.

cases.problem: PRECISION (severe). Token && overlap (solgar OR magnezijum OR mg) returns ~4286 rows. Intended intent — Solgar-brand magnesium — is only ~69 products (title has both 'solgar' AND 'magn'). Because ANY token suffices, the brand and the ingredient are OR'd, so the whole Solgar catalog (724) and all magnesium/mg products flood in.
cases.expected: Return the ~69 Solgar magnesium products at top (brand AND ingredient must both match — AND semantics, not OR). Non-Solgar magnesium and non-magnesium Solgar items must rank far below or be excluded. This is the canonical brand+ingredient AND-intent regression.

cases.problem: PRECISION. Multi-word strength query. Token overlap (vitamin OR c OR 1000) returns ~3537 vs the ~238 titles that actually contain 'vitamin c' AND '1000'. The number token '1000' alone is on 1061 products (any 1000mg/1000IU/1000ml item), and 'c' is a wildcard, so strength and ingredient are not jointly enforced.
cases.expected: Return Vitamin C products at the 1000 mg strength ('BIOFAR Vitamin C 1000', 'HealthAid Vitamin C 1000mg'). Ingredient (Vitamin C) AND strength (1000) must both bind. Must NOT return Vitamin D3 1000IU, Calcium 1000, or random 1000-count products. Under the new group key this maps to group 'Vitamin C :: 1000 :: mg'.

cases.problem: PRECISION (extreme) + stopword. Token overlap krema-OR-lice returns ~9510 (8581 just from token 'krema'); true 'krema za lice' substring is only ~628. 'krema' is a FORM (8322 products), not an ingredient, and 'za' is a stopword. Treating a category/form phrase like an ingredient query returns nearly every cream in the catalog.
cases.expected: Return face creams specifically (titles containing 'za lice'/'for face' or face-category creams), ~600s scale, not all 8500 creams. 'za' must be dropped as a stopword. Form 'krema' should filter/category-facet, while 'lice' (face) narrows it. This is the category-query regression: form words must not behave like ingredient matches.

cases.problem: RECALL (typo, missing 'j'). ilike '%magnezium%' hits only 6. Trigram p.title % 'magnezium' returns 33 because real titles are long and whole-title similarity falls under the 0.3 limit (e.g. 'Dr Lukić Magnezijum Hlorid gel 180ml' similarity 0.10). 648 genuine magnezijum products are missed entirely by both ilike and trigram-on-full-title.
cases.expected: A one-character typo of a common ingredient must still return the ~900 magnesium products. Fuzzy matching needs to operate on the ingredient/coreProductIdentity token, not whole long titles. coreProductIdentity 'Magnezijum'/'Magnesium' should be reachable from 'magnezium'.

cases.problem: RECALL. Expansion zn->'zinc' (English) but DB is overwhelmingly Serbian 'cink' (588) vs 'zinc' (176). The zn-token-OR-zinc path yields ~391; true zinc intent (cink+zinc) is ~755. The English-only expansion target systematically misses Serbian-spelled minerals.
cases.expected: 'zn' must return zinc/cink products (cink AND zinc unified) — ~755 scale. coreProductIdentity must canonicalize 'Cink' and 'Zinc' to one ingredient. Same class of bug as fe->iron (misses 'gvožđe', 95 products) and mag->magnesium.

cases.problem: PRECISION (substring) + grouping. ilike '%cink%' (588) over-broadens vs exact token 'cink' (475): it catches 'Cinkdermin pasta', 'krema sa cinkom', 'BebiCol sa cinkom' — zinc-containing creams/drops where zinc is an additive, not the product identity. Substring matching conflates the mineral supplement with topical/additive products.
cases.expected: Top results are zinc supplements (coreProductIdentity 'Cink'/'Zinc'). Zinc-as-additive cosmetics/drops ('krema sa cinkom') may appear but must not outrank or dominate pure-zinc supplements. Must not match unrelated words via substring. coreProductIdentity must not be 'Cinkdermin'.

cases.problem: GROUPING noise (drives both search ranking and the new group key). 121 distinct coreProductIdentity values contain 'vitamin d3' — leaks include 'Vitamin D3 90Kap', 'Vitamin D3 I.U', 'Vitamin D3 Šumeće', 'Three Vitamin D3', 'Nutrition Vitamin D3', 'Citrat Vitamin D3', '7Nutrition Vitamin D3'. Pack size, unit ('i.u'), form ('šumeće'), and leftover brand/variant tokens contaminate the ingredient identity, fragmenting one group into 121.
cases.expected: All plain Vitamin D3 products collapse to coreProductIdentity 'Vitamin D3' regardless of brand/pack/form. 'Vitamin D3 90Kap'->'Vitamin D3', 'Nutrition/7Nutrition/Three Vitamin D3'->'Vitamin D3', 'Vitamin D3 I.U'->'Vitamin D3'. Combos that change the ingredient (D3+K2, Calcium+D3) stay distinct. Group key = 'Vitamin D3' + strength only.

cases.problem: PRECISION/strength binding. Combines the bare-number wildcard problems: token '3' (2136) and token '1000' (1061) both behave as wildcards, while 'omega' is the only real constraint. Returns thousands. Strength is also poorly populated, so binding omega-3 to a 1000mg strength is unreliable.
cases.expected: Return omega-3 products at 1000 mg strength. Numbers must bind as ingredient-strength, not free-floating tokens. Under the new group key: 'Omega 3 :: 1000 :: mg'. Must not return Vitamin D3 1000IU or any 1000-count item.

cases.problem: RECALL via expansion gap. Expansion 'prob'->'probiotic' only fires on the exact token 'prob', never on the real Serbian word 'probiotik' or 'probiotske'. ilike '%probiot%' is the only thing covering all forms (~361). Without a stem, 'probiotik' (SR), 'probiotic' (EN), 'probiotske kapi' are not unified by expansion.
cases.expected: 'probiotik' returns all probiotic products (probiotik/probiotic/probiotske unified, ~361). coreProductIdentity canonicalizes to 'Probiotik'. Serbian and English spellings plus adjectival forms ('probiotske') match the same ingredient.

cases.problem: BASELINE/control (Serbian ingredient with NO expansion entry). 364 'selen' products. ilike works, but there's no synonym handling: 'selenium' (EN) titles would be missed, and there is no abbreviation. Useful as a regression that a clean single Serbian ingredient returns a tight, correct set.
cases.expected: Return the ~364 selenium products, coreProductIdentity 'Selen' (unify any 'Selenium' EN spelling). Tight precision — no leakage from 'sel'-substring or unrelated tokens. Serves as the positive-control assertion that simple ingredient search stays clean.

cases.problem: GROUPING noise + EN/SR. 1059 collagen products but coreProductIdentity fragments: 'Kolagen'(38), 'Kolagen Beauty', 'Tečni Kolagen', 'Kolagen Prašak Kesicama Paket', 'Kolagen Peptan Vitamin C', plus English 'collagen'/'Colnatur' variants. Form ('tečni'/liquid, 'prašak'/powder), marketing ('Beauty'), pack ('Paket') and co-ingredients leak into the identity.
cases.expected: Plain collagen products collapse to coreProductIdentity 'Kolagen' (unify 'Collagen'). Strip form ('tečni', 'prašak'), pack ('paket', 'kesicama'), and marketing ('beauty'). Collagen+VitaminC combos are a distinct ingredient identity. Liquid vs powder become offers/forms within the group, not separate groups.

cases.problem: PRECISION (multi-ingredient). 112 titles have both magnezijum AND b6, but token overlap (magnezijum OR b6 OR mg) returns thousands. Identity is also inconsistent: 'Magnezijum B6'(36), 'Magnezijum Vitamin B6'(11), 'Magnezijum Vitaminom B6'(9), 'Magnezijum Direkt B C'(6) — the same combo splits across multiple identity strings.
cases.expected: Return the magnesium+B6 combination products (~112). Both ingredients must bind (AND). 'Magnezijum B6' / 'Magnezijum Vitamin B6' / 'Magnezijum Vitaminom B6' must canonicalize to one identity. Must NOT return plain magnesium or plain B6 as exact matches, and must NOT flood via the 'mg' dosage token.

cases.problem: PRECISION + form-as-token. 'serum' is a form word; token overlap pulls every serum plus every 'c'/'vitamin' product. Real intent (Vitamin C facial serum) is a small set, but 'c' wildcard + 'serum' form blow up results. coreProductIdentity for these is noisy: 'Redermic Pure Vitamin C', 'Termalna Vitamin Cg'.
cases.expected: Return Vitamin C serums (cosmetic), ingredient 'Vitamin C' bound + form/category 'serum' as a filter, not a free token. Identity must strip brand line ('Redermic'), fix mis-parse ('Vitamin Cg'->'Vitamin C'). Under new design, form (serum) is an offer/facet within the 'Vitamin C' group, so this validates form is excluded from the key but usable as filter.

cases.problem: PRECISION/intent. Brand-only query. Token 'solgar' on 724 products. With && overlap a single-token brand query is fine for recall, but ranking has no notion that this is a brand browse — it interleaves by trigram similarity and price rather than presenting the brand's catalog grouped by ingredient.
cases.expected: Return Solgar-branded products (~724), ideally grouped/faceted by ingredient. extractedBrand='Solgar' is the binding field. Must not return non-Solgar products via fuzzy trigram (e.g. 'solgar' must not trigram-match unrelated long titles below intent). Validates brand-only browse behavior.

cases.problem: RECALL (SR/EN + spacing). Intent is B-complex; DB has 'b complex', 'b-complex', 'b kompleks', 'b-kompleks' (~268 total across spellings). No expansion entry unifies Serbian 'kompleks' with English 'complex', and hyphen/space variants tokenize differently ([b,kompleks] vs [b,complex]). Single 'b' token is also dropped/ambiguous.
cases.expected: 'b kompleks' returns B-complex products across 'complex'/'kompleks' and hyphen/space variants (~268). coreProductIdentity canonicalizes to 'B Kompleks'/'B Complex' as one ingredient. Must not collapse into individual B-vitamins (B6/B12) or generic 'vitamin b'.

cases.problem: RECALL (typo, transposed/missing letter in 'vitamin'). ilike '%vitmin%' hits only 2. Trigram 'vitmin c' on full titles returns ~35 — far below the ~960 real Vitamin C products, because whole-title similarity to a 7-char query is tiny for long product titles. Common misspelling effectively returns almost nothing.
cases.expected: A typo of 'vitamin' must still surface Vitamin C products at meaningful recall. Fuzzy matching should target the ingredient/identity tokens, not whole titles. 'vitmin c' should behave close to 'vitamin c'. Regression guards against trigram-on-long-title recall collapse for misspelled ingredients.

cases.problem: RECALL + diacritics + EN/SR. Iron in Serbian is 'gvožđe'/'gvozdje' (~95) while expansion fe->iron targets English 'iron' (212). Diacritics (ž, đ) must normalize to z/dj. There is no unification of 'gvožđe', 'gvozdje', 'iron', 'Fe' into one ingredient, so an iron query in any single spelling misses the others.
cases.expected: 'gvožđe' (and 'gvozdje', 'fe', 'iron') all return iron supplements; diacritic-insensitive. coreProductIdentity unifies Iron/Gvožđe/Fe into one ingredient (~300 across spellings). Validates Serbian diacritic normalization and SR/EN ingredient synonymy.

cases.problem: GROUPING/ranking. 'omega' alone is broad; identity fragments into 'Omega'(143), 'Omega3'(19), 'Omega Fish'(7), 'Dha Omega'(7), 'Omega Vitamin E'(6), 'Omega 90Kap'(5), 'Omega Double Strenght'(4). Pack/form/marketing tokens ('90Kap','Double Strenght','Meke Želatinozne Paket') contaminate identity, so even a clean prefix query returns a fragmented group set.
cases.expected: 'omega' returns omega-fatty-acid products grouped under canonical 'Omega 3' (and distinct 'Omega 3-6-9' where applicable). Strip pack ('90Kap','Paket'), form ('Meke Želatinozne'), and marketing ('Double Strenght'). Co-ingredient combos (Omega+Vitamin E) are distinct identities. Validates de-fragmentation of the omega group.

cases.problem: PRECISION/category. Pure form word: token 'krema' is on 8581 products (form='krema' on 8322). As an ingredient-style search it returns essentially the entire cream catalog with no narrowing, and trigram/price ranking gives an arbitrary order. Form must be a facet/category, not an ingredient match.
cases.expected: 'krema' is treated as a form/category browse: return creams grouped by their actual ingredient/identity (and faceted), not 8500 ungrouped rows ranked by price. Confirms that under the new design FORM is excluded from the group key and instead acts as a category/offer dimension.

search.notes:
Source of truth: searchProductsDB() in /Users/ahab/pharma-search/main.go (lines 76-144) and helpers Tokenize/ExpandQueryVariants/NormalizeText in /Users/ahab/pharma-search/internal/matching/matching.go (lines 169-264). All counts below are from the live local DB (104,780 products, 80 vendors) via the provided PSQL helper.

ROOT-CAUSE PATTERNS (each gold case maps to one or more):

1) OR-semantics token overlap is the dominant PRECISION bug. The WHERE clause uses `searchTokens && q.tokens` (matching.go Tokenize -> array; main.go line 113), and `&&` means ANY shared token qualifies. So multi-word queries become OR queries. Measured blowups: 'vitamin c' 2912 vs 960 true; 'solgar magnezijum' 4286 vs 69 true; 'omega 3' 2304 vs 450 true; 'vitamin c 1000' 3537 vs 238 true; 'krema za lice' 9510 vs 628 true. Fix direction: require AND/`@>` (all query tokens present) for multi-token queries, or weight `@>` matches far above `&&`. Note the ORDER BY already has a `@>` tier (line 129) but it only ranks, it does not filter.

2) Single-character and bare-number tokens act as wildcards. Tokenize() whitelists 'd','b','c','k' (matching.go line 205) and keeps all digit tokens. Token 'c' is on 1793 products, 'd' on 700, '3' on 2136, '1000' on 1061. These should never be standalone match drivers; they must only count when bound to an ingredient (e.g. 'vitamin'+'c', 'vitamin'+'d3', 'omega'+'3').

3) Dosage-unit/strength tokens collide with ingredients. 'mg' appears as a searchToken on 3006 non-magnesium products (strengths like 500mg). Any magnesium path keying on 'mg' is wrong. Keep strength tokens out of ingredient-match space (they belong to the dosage facet / group key, not free-text tokens).

4) EN-only query expansion misses Serbian titles (RECALL). queryExpansions (matching.go ~line 94) maps mag->magnesium, zn->zinc, fe->iron, calc->calcium, prob->probiotic — but DB titles are mostly Serbian: magnezijum 669 vs magnesium 277; cink 588 vs zinc 176; gvožđe/gvozdje 95 vs iron 212. Expansions must target the Serbian forms (or, better, canonicalize both SR and EN ingredient names to one identity). Also expansion only fires on exact tokens, so the full Serbian word ('magnezijum','probiotik') gets no synonym treatment.

5) Trigram `%` on whole long titles collapses typo recall. show_limit()=0.3. Typo 'magnezium' (missing j): ilike hits 6, title%query hits 33, but 648 real magnezijum products are missed because whole-title similarity to a short query is ~0.10 for long titles. 'vitmin c' typo: only ~35 of ~960. Fuzzy matching should run against coreProductIdentity / ingredient tokens, not full titles.

6) Identity noise/fragmentation directly degrades both grouping AND search ranking (coreProductIdentity is matched and ranked, main.go lines 111/124/128/131/138). Examples: 121 distinct coreProductIdentity values contain 'vitamin d3' (leaks: '90Kap','I.U','Šumeće','Three','Nutrition','Citrat','7Nutrition'); omega split across Omega/Omega3/Omega 90Kap/Dha Omega/Omega Double Strenght; magnesium combo split across 'Magnezijum B6'/'Magnezijum Vitamin B6'/'Magnezijum Vitaminom B6'; kolagen split by form ('Tečni','Prašak') and marketing ('Beauty'). The extractor (ml/populate_missing_data.py _extract_core_ingredient) must strip pack size (NNkap/Kap/Paket/Kesicama), units (i.u/i.j/iu/mg/ml), form words (šumeće/tečni/prašak/serum/krema), brand/line tokens (Nutrition/7Nutrition/Three/Professional Vitamins/Redermic), and unify SR<->EN ingredient spellings.

COVERAGE STATS confirming the brief: dosageValue present on only 4.88% of rows; form on 41.1%. On the 431 'vitamin d3' titles only 270 have a dosage — so strength-aware grouping/search will be weak until dosage extraction improves; the gold set includes strength-binding cases ('vitamin c 1000','omega 3 1000') as forward-looking regressions.

HOW TO USE AS REGRESSION ASSERTIONS: for each case, assert (a) top-K precision: the first N results' coreProductIdentity equals the expected canonical ingredient; (b) the listed must-NOT items are absent from top-K; (c) recall floor: result count is within a sane band of the 'true' ilike count cited per case (not the inflated && count). The 'true' counts above are the intended targets; the inflated counts are the current behavior to drive down.

overMergeRisks: CROSS-CATEGORY COLLISION via 'q10': proposed group key=`q10` (no dosage on any row) merges 125+ offers across totally different product classes: CoQ10 SUPPLEMENT capsules (Dietpharm Koenzim Q10, Oligovit Q10, CO Q10 200mg x30 kapsule), Q10 FACE CREAMS (NIVEA VISAGE Q10 NOCNA KREMA 50ML, Eucerin Q10 Active eye cream, SEBAMED Q10 krema 50ml), Q10 LOSION, even a SCHAUMA Q10 shampoo and a Baroness Q10 hair MASK. ~52 krema + 129 kapsule + 6 losion + masks all collapse into ONE group. This is the single most damaging case.

GENERIC ADJECTIVE/EFFECT CORE 'control': key=`control` merges 142 offers / 36 brands / 9 forms: A-Derma Exomega Control krema/mleko/ulje/kupka (multiple volumes), Avene Tolerance Control krema+balzam, Urriage Toleederm Control eye cream, AND an unrelated supplement 'Elementum Spike Control 60 kapsula'. Distinct product lines + a supplement all in one bucket.

MICELLAR WATER 'micelarna': key=`micelarna` merges 104 offers / 48 brands / 0 dosage: Bioderma Hydrabio/Sensibio/Sebium, Neutrogena Detox, Mustela PhysiObebe, NIVEA Soothing, Babe Essentials, Eucerin DermatoCLEAN — every brand AND every volume (100/200/250/400/500ml) collapses to one group.

ANTI-DANDRUFF SHAMPOO 'protiv peruti': key=`protiv peruti` merges 117 offers across 66 distinct brands and many volumes — essentially the entire anti-dandruff shampoo category becomes a single group. Sibling 'protiv opadanja' (anti-hairloss) = 129 offers / 65 brands mixing sampon+losion+tonik (Pantogar, Phyto, Fitoval, Rigenforte, Apivita...).

PANTHENOL CROSS-FORM 'pantenol': key=`pantenol` (114 offers, 25 brands) merges Pantenol pena 9%, sprej 10%, rastvor, krem-gel, emulzija, MASKA za lice, and even 'Afrodita Kopriva i Pantenol sampon 1000ml' — wound foam + face mask + shampoo in one group.

BARE FORM / POWDER 'prasak': key=`prasak` (166-192 offers, 112 brands, no dose) merges every unrelated sachet/powder: Soda bikarbona, D-Manoza, Sideral folico (iron), Probiokid, Magnewill, Geladrink Ferritin — ingredient identity is completely lost.

GENERIC CREAM-DESCRIPTOR CORES from face creams that reduce to an adjective: `uree`(114)/`urea`(47) [SAME urea creams under two cores], `hydra`(40), `zastitna`(35), `obnavljajuca`(31), `hidratantna`(24), `bogata`(26), `cold`(23), `soft`(67), `cica`(68) — each merges multiple brands and volumes (e.g. uree: Sebamed/Eucerin/Atopik at 50/75/100/200/250ml) into one undifferentiated group.

SCALE: 1,974 doseless proposed groups have >=5 offers AND >=3 distinct brands, covering 28,153 offers (~27% of the 104,780-row catalog) — these are the population most likely to be wrong merges since with no dosage the key is just the (often generic) core. 32,524 doseless offers have a single-token core.
underMergeRisks: ALIAS NOT MAPPED (Latin/English vs Serbian): `magnezijum`(77) vs `magnesium`(30); `magnezijum citrat`(21) vs `magnesium citrate`(15); `magnezijum bisglicinat`(17) vs `magnesium bisglycinate`(9). Same ingredient, different cores -> separate groups. Brand strip-list of 583 does not normalize these ingredient aliases.

DOSE-UNIT EQUIVALENCE NOT NORMALIZED: vitamin d3 splits by unit iu(169) / mcg(4) / mg(1). 10 mcg == 400 IU pharmacologically, but `vitamin d3::dose:10mcg` and `vitamin d3::dose:400iu` form different keys -> identical products split. (Also the lone 500mg row is a mis-extraction that creates a junk singleton group.)

MISSING DOSAGE ON MOST OFFERS: only 5,109/104,780 rows (4.9%) have any dosage. For 'vitamin d3' core, 32 of 206 rows have NO dose, so a 2000 IU D3 with the dose stripped falls into the bare `vitamin d3` group while its dosed twin sits in `vitamin d3::dose:2000iu` — same product, two groups. With 95.1% of catalog doseless, dosage cannot be relied on as a discriminator anywhere.

CORE FRAGMENTATION FROM LEAKED NOISE: 'vitamin d3' canonical ingredient is scattered across `vitamin d3`(187), `vitamin d3 90kap`(6), `vitamin d3 sumecih`(6), `vitamin d3 i.u`(4), `three vitamin d3`(5), `citrat vitamin d3`(4), `vitamin d3+k2`/`vitamin d3 k2`(28) — leftover quantity/unit/Serbian-form/brand tokens prevent merge of the same strength.

MULTI-INGREDIENT COMBO COLLAPSE OF ORDER/ALIAS: 'magnezijum + b6' fragments into 40+ cores: `magnezijum b6`(36), `magnezijum vitamin b6`(11), `magnezijum vitaminom b6`(9), `magnezijum vit b6`(4), `magnezan b6`(2), `mineral magnezijum b6`, `aktiv magnezijum b6 b12` etc. No canonical ordering/alias of combo ingredients -> the largest combo category never merges.

SALT-FORM TREATED AS PART OF CORE: magnezijum citrat / glicinat / bisglicinat / malat / hlorid are separate cores. If the redesign intends these as one 'magnezijum' group they under-merge; if intended as distinct they actually need form-class kept (see safeguards) — current extraction does neither consistently.
regressionQueries: Over-merge canary (must DROP after fix): WITH g AS (SELECT lower(trim("coreProductIdentity")) core FROM "Product" WHERE "dosageValue" IS NULL AND "coreProductIdentity" IS NOT NULL AND trim("coreProductIdentity")<>'') SELECT count(*) susp_groups, sum(offers) susp_offers FROM (SELECT core, count(*) offers, count(DISTINCT "extractedBrand") b FROM "Product" p JOIN g USING (true) GROUP BY core) s WHERE offers>=5 AND b>=3;  -- baseline before: 1974 groups / 28153 offers. (Simplify by grouping Product directly on lower(trim(core)) where dose is null.)

Single-token generic core count (baseline 32524, must drop): SELECT count(*) FROM "Product" WHERE "dosageValue" IS NULL AND "coreProductIdentity" IS NOT NULL AND array_length(regexp_split_to_array(trim("coreProductIdentity"),'\s+'),1)=1;

Bare form/generic-word core groups (must -> 0 real groups; should fall back to per-offer key): SELECT lower(trim("coreProductIdentity")) core, count(*) FROM "Product" WHERE lower(trim("coreProductIdentity")) IN ('krema','krem','serum','gel','sampon','sampon','losion','maska','ulje','voda','prasak','prah','sprej','kapi','mleko','control','protect','sensitive','fresh','repair','soft','hydra','cica','micelarna','pena','tonik') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC;  -- baseline: prasak 192, control 142, sensitive 104, micelarna 104, protect 82, fresh 81, repair 80, cica 68, soft 67, hydra 49.

q10 cross-category split check (after fix CoQ10 supplement and Q10 cream must be in DIFFERENT groups): SELECT form, count(*) FROM "Product" WHERE lower(trim("coreProductIdentity"))='q10' GROUP BY form;  -- baseline collapses kapsule(129)+krema(52)+losion(6)+gel(1)+NULL(35)+tablete(15) into one key.

D3 alias+unit consolidation (after fix should converge): SELECT lower(trim("coreProductIdentity")) core, "dosageUnit", count(*) FROM "Product" WHERE lower(title) ~ '(vitamin\s*d3|\bd3\b)' GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 3 DESC;  -- baseline core fragments: vitamin d3(187), vitamin d3 90kap(6), three vitamin d3(5), citrat vitamin d3(4), vitamin d3 i.u(4); units iu/mcg/mg split.

magnezijum+b6 combo fragmentation (count of distinct cores must drop sharply): SELECT count(DISTINCT lower(trim("coreProductIdentity"))) FROM "Product" WHERE lower(title) ~ 'magnez' AND lower(title) ~ 'b\s*6|b6';  -- baseline 40+ distinct cores.

Group-count delta sanity: WITH base AS (SELECT lower(trim("coreProductIdentity")) c, CASE WHEN "dosageValue" IS NOT NULL AND "dosageUnit" IS NOT NULL THEN '::d'||"dosageValue"||lower("dosageUnit") ELSE '' END d FROM "Product" WHERE "coreProductIdentity" IS NOT NULL AND trim("coreProductIdentity")<>'') SELECT count(DISTINCT c||d) FROM base;  -- baseline proposed=50277; total Product=104780; multi-vendor groups only 9976 (track that legit multi-vendor groups stay >= baseline while junk groups shrink).

Empty-core fallback audit: SELECT count(*) FROM "Product" WHERE "coreProductIdentity" IS NULL OR trim("coreProductIdentity")='';  -- baseline 53; verify these never join a shared group (Go BuildGroupID returns '' and currently falls back to extractGroupKey(title)).
audit.verdict: GROUP-BY (canonical ingredient + strength) is sound IN PRINCIPLE only for the ~4.9% of rows that have a dosage AND a clean ingredient core. Against real data it is currently UNSAFE: 95.1% of the catalog (99,671 rows) has no dosage, so for them the key degenerates to the bare core, which today is frequently a generic adjective/effect word ('control','sensitive','protect','micelarna','protiv peruti','q10','prasak','uree'). Simulating the exact proposed key (matching.BuildGroupID = NormalizeText(core)::dose, form/vol/qty excluded) shows ~1,974 doseless groups with >=5 offers and >=3 brands spanning 28,153 offers (27% of catalog) that wrongly merge distinct products, including a cross-category 'q10' bucket mixing CoQ10 supplement capsules with Q10 face creams, a losion, a shampoo and a hair mask. Meanwhile genuine merges still fail (D3 fragments into 10+ cores and splits on iu/mcg; magnezijum+b6 fragments into 40+ cores; magnezijum vs magnesium alias split). The proposed key yields only 9,976 multi-vendor groups out of 50,277 — most groups gain no comparison value while the dense ones over-merge. MUST-HAVE SAFEGUARDS: (1) Maintain a whitelist of canonical ingredients (the ~60 miners found, plus Serbian/Latin/English aliases AND combo-ingredient normalization with sorted multi-ingredient sets e.g. magnezijum+b6); ONLY collapse to a shared ingredient+strength group when the core maps to a whitelisted canonical ingredient. (2) NEVER merge when the core is empty, a single bare form/generic word (krema/serum/control/q10/prasak/micelarna...), or not on the whitelist — fall back to a per-offer key (title-derived, so each offer stays its own 'group'); do not let unmatched rows pool. (3) Normalize dose UNITS to a canonical scale before keying (iu<->mcg for D3: 1mcg=40IU) and drop implausible dose extractions (D3 in mg). (4) For non-dosage products (cosmetics), strength is meaningless — keep a coarse FORM-CLASS in the key (and ideally brand+line for cosmetics) instead of stripping it, since these products are differentiated by brand/line/volume not by an ingredient identity; otherwise they should NOT be auto-merged at all. (5) Strip leaked noise (90kap, i.u, sumecih, salt-form words, count tokens) from core before keying. (6) Add a guard that refuses any group exceeding a brand-diversity threshold without a dosage (e.g. >3 brands and no dose -> treat as suspect, split by brand/form). Gate the rollout on the regression queries above (suspicious-group count and single-token-core count must fall sharply; multi-vendor legit groups must not).
